briefing.incidentSummary:
Rohan Verma, a senior developer at DataCore Solutions, resigned suddenly — skipping the standard two-week notice and leaving the same day.

When IT reviewed his workstation, several critical project files were missing. System logs confirmed the files were deleted shortly before his resignation.

Fortunately, a full forensic disk image was captured before the machine was reassigned.

The question is: were those files permanently wiped — or can they still be recovered?
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Scan the disk image for deleted file fragments

› Reconstruct the deleted documents from recovered fragments

› Build the deletion timeline to prove intentional removal

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Rohan Verma

suspicionTrack.reason: Primary user of the workstation — all file activity maps to his session
Deleted files directly linked to his active session just before shutdown
Deletion timeline proves intentional removal minutes before departure

digitalFootprint.deviceUsage: Dev Workstation WS-07 — sole assigned user

suspects.profileNotes: Sole assigned user of workstation WS-07. His final login session ran from 18:30 to 18:52. During this session, confidential project files were accessed at 18:42, deleted at 18:47, and the system was shut down at 18:52. The rapid sequence — access, delete, shut down — is strongly consistent with deliberate data destruction prior to departure. No other user accessed the workstation in this window.

suspects.name: IT Staff

suspicionTrack.reason: Has system-level access to all workstations
No after-hours activity recorded on WS-07
No direct evidence of involvement — deletion maps exclusively to Rohan's session

digitalFootprint.deviceUsage: IT Admin Console — no remote session to WS-07 in evening

suspects.profileNotes: Has administrative access to workstations for maintenance purposes. However, no remote session or admin login to WS-07 is recorded during the evening window. Correctly followed forensic protocol by capturing a disk image before reassignment. No motive identified.

suspects.name: External Actor

suspicionTrack.reason: Remote intrusion cannot be immediately ruled out
No inbound network connections detected on WS-07 during the session
Network logs confirm no external access — local user action only

digitalFootprint.ipActivity: No external IPs detected on WS-07
digitalFootprint.deviceUsage: No remote desktop or VPN session recorded
digitalFootprint.vpnCheck: No VPN detected on target machine

suspects.profileNotes: Network traffic logs show no inbound connections to WS-07 during the final session. No RDP, SSH, or VPN tunnels were active. The deletion was performed locally by the logged-in user — Rohan Verma.

suspects.name: Insider Collaborator

suspicionTrack.reason: Possible that Rohan acted on behalf of a competitor or internal contact
Recovered documents include competitor-sensitive roadmap data
No direct evidence of a collaborator — Rohan acted alone on the workstation

suspicionTrack.label: Moderate — possible motive link, no direct evidence

suspects.profileNotes: It is possible Rohan was acting in coordination with a competitor or had already exfiltrated the data before deleting it. However, investigation of WS-07 alone cannot confirm this. The deletion is attributable to Rohan regardless of motive or collaboration.

items.label: Disk Image — Unallocated Space Analysis
items.detail: The forensic disk image of workstation WS-07 (System Drive C: — 500 GB) reveals significant unallocated space containing data remnants. When files are deleted on a standard Windows NTFS system, the file system marks the storage blocks as available but does not immediately overwrite the data. Analysis of unallocated sectors identifies three recoverable file fragments matching the names, sizes, and internal metadata of the missing project documents: Client_Report.docx, Architecture_Diagram.pdf, and Strategy_Presentation.pptx. The data is intact — the files were deleted using the standard Windows delete function, not a secure wipe utility.

minigame.title: Unallocated Space Scanner
minigame.description: Scan the disk image sectors and identify which blocks still contain deleted file fragments. Highlight the sectors showing residual data from the three missing documents.
minigame.hint: Look for sectors with recognisable file signatures — DOCX starts with PK, PDF starts with %PDF.
hints: Three of the six sectors contain fragments. Look for file magic bytes in the hint column.
Sectors 0x0041A3, 0x008C11, and 0x00FF3E each show residual file data.

onSuccess.message: FRAGMENTS LOCATED — All three deleted files exist in unallocated space. The data was never securely wiped. Proceeding to reconstruction.

items.label: Recovered File Records — Deletion Confirmed
items.detail: Master File Table (MFT) analysis confirms three files were deleted from the workstation during Rohan Verma's final session. The MFT retains metadata for deleted files including original filename, size, creation date, last modified date, and deletion timestamp. Client_Report.docx — deleted 18:47. Architecture_Diagram.pdf — deleted 18:47. Strategy_Presentation.pptx — deleted 18:47. All three files were deleted in the same 60-second window, strongly indicating a batch deletion action rather than accidental removal. File content is recoverable from unallocated space.

rows.value: Standard Windows delete — not securely wiped

rows.key: Active User at Time of Deletion

minigame.title: Deletion Timeline Builder
minigame.description: Plot the key events from Rohan's final session onto the timeline in the correct order. Arrange: file access, batch deletion, and system shutdown to prove the sequence was intentional.
minigame.hint: Arrange the four events from earliest to latest using the timestamps.
hints: The session starts at 18:30 and ends at 18:52 with shutdown. Place login first and shutdown last.
The sequence is: Login (18:30) → Access files (18:42) → Delete files (18:47) → Shutdown (18:52).

onSuccess.message: TIMELINE CONFIRMED — Files were accessed, then deleted, then the machine was shut down — all within 22 minutes. This is not accidental deletion. This is deliberate data destruction immediately before departure.

items.label: Recovered Document Contents — Confidential Project Data
items.detail: Forensic reconstruction of the three recovered files confirms their contents. Client_Report.docx contained a completed deliverable for a major client including financial projections and proprietary methodology. Architecture_Diagram.pdf contained the system architecture for an unreleased product feature. Strategy_Presentation.pptx contained a 3-year business roadmap marked CONFIDENTIAL — INTERNAL ONLY. All three documents are classified as trade secrets under the company's data classification policy. Their deletion immediately before Rohan's departure — without authorisation — constitutes a potential breach of his employment contract and applicable data protection law.

rows.value: Trade secrets — protected under employment contract

timeline.title: Rohan Works on Confidential Projects
timeline.description: Rohan Verma regularly accesses Client_Report, Architecture_Diagram, and Strategy_Presentation as part of his role.

timeline.title: Final Session Begins
timeline.description: Rohan logs into WS-07. This is his last recorded login session.

timeline.title: Confidential Files Accessed
timeline.description: All three project files are opened during the final session.

timeline.title: Files Deleted — Batch
timeline.description: All three files deleted within a 60-second window using standard Windows delete.

timeline.title: Workstation Shut Down
timeline.description: Rohan shuts down the workstation and departs the building.

timeline.title: IT Captures Disk Image
timeline.description: IT department creates forensic disk image before reassigning the workstation. Deleted fragments preserved.

timeline.title: Files Successfully Recovered
timeline.description: Forensic analysis recovers all three documents from unallocated space.

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Rohan Verma confirmed — deliberately deleted confidential project files minutes before resigning.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect, but more forensic evidence is needed to build the full picture.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The deletion maps exclusively to Rohan Verma's final session on WS-07.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

coldCase.title: Case Gone Cold
coldCase.subtitle: No evidence collected. The deletion goes unexplained.
coldCase.label: COLD CASE

aria.welcomeToHub:
...He left in a day. The files left with him.
Except — deleted doesn't always mean gone.
The disk remembers everything.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Unallocated sectors. MFT records. A 22-minute window.
Something was erased in a hurry. Find out what — and prove who.
aria.viewEvidence:
Three files. One session. A shutdown five minutes later.
This wasn't accidental. Prove it.
aria.markEvidence: Good. The fragments are forming a picture.
aria.decryptionHint: Scan the sectors — look for file signatures in the unallocated blocks.
aria.flagSuspect:
The timeline doesn't leave room for doubt.
Access. Delete. Shutdown. All in 22 minutes.
Who was logged in?
